calendar.description: Senate Transportation, Public Utilities, Energy, and Technology Hearing

general_provisions: This bill amends license plate provisions, repeals the clean vehicle program, amends distribution frequency for class B and class C road funding, and amends provisions related to local corridor preservation fund administration.
hilighted_provisions: This bill:<hr><ltbullet>amends provisions related to local jurisdiction over real property as pertaining to commuter rail property;<hr><ltbullet>repeals the clean vehicle program;<hr><ltbullet>defines terms related to license plates;<hr><ltbullet>changes the process for the design and issuance of standard license plates;<hr><ltbullet>amends the process for redesign of standard license plate options;<hr><ltbullet>amends provisions related to the visibility and readability of license plates, including replacement of faded license plates;<hr><ltbullet>changes the process for creation of a new special group license plate and eligibility of the sponsor;<hr><ltbullet>requires the Motor Vehicle Division to establish certain service fees related to license plate administration;<hr><ltbullet>provides a process to transfer control and revenue related to special group license plates;<hr><ltbullet>amends the design and approval process for special group license plates;<hr><ltbullet>amends provisions related to the design review board, including duties and membership;<hr><ltbullet>amends provisions related to the State Tax Commission's contract with a license plate designer;<hr><ltbullet>requires driver license and identification card designs to be approved by the design review board;<hr><ltbullet>amends certain duties of the Department of Transportation;<hr><ltbullet>amends rates related to the road usage charge program;<hr><ltbullet>enacts provisions related to privileged information pertaining to rail road safety responsibilities and coordination;<hr><ltbullet>provides flexibility in distribution frequency of class B and class C road funding; <hr><ltbullet>directs local corridor preservation funds to go directly to the relevant local government instead of passing through the Transportation Fund;<hr><ltbullet>modifies certain uses for local corridor preservation funds; and<hr><ltbullet>makes technical changes.
